metadata.title: LLMPerf Cost Model Architecture
metadata.subtitle: An interactive map of workload, hardware, operator, communication, memory, search, and scenario analyses.
metadata.paperTitle: LLMPerf Cost Model Architecture: A Hardware-Aware Analytical Framework for Large Language Model Inference
metadata.objective: Rapid, explainable design-space exploration for LLM inference architecture, model-system co-design, and deployment planning.
metadata.scope: Analytical estimates for workload structure, hardware abstraction, operator timing, communication, memory capacity, strategy search, scenario studies, outputs, and model limits.
metadata.recommendedWorkflow: Use the model to narrow a large architecture space, validate selected points with kernel and communication benchmarks, then feed calibration back into hardware tables and transport constants.

audiences.label: Architecture / Hardware
audiences.summary: Hardware abstraction, NoC traffic, memory capacity, and multi-GPU mapping.

audiences.label: Model / Algorithm
audiences.summary: Workload structure, attention, MoE, MTP, KV cache, and phase behavior.

audiences.label: Business / Planning
audiences.summary: Throughput, latency, Pareto choices, capacity planning, and bottleneck explanations.

audiences.label: Paper / External
audiences.summary: Methodology, assumptions, analysis scope, and limitations suitable for a paper narrative.

architectureFlow.title: Workload Inputs
architectureFlow.summary: Model layers, phase, sequence lengths, precision, batch, and model configuration.

architectureFlow.title: Hardware Abstraction
architectureFlow.summary: GPU topology, compute tiles, bandwidths, latencies, capacities, and system maps.

architectureFlow.title: Operator Models
architectureFlow.summary: GEMM, MHA, softmax, vector, and depthwise kernels modeled at SM/GPU granularity.

architectureFlow.title: Communication and NoC
architectureFlow.summary: TP, EP, PP, CP communication plus on-chip traffic through SM/GPC/L2/HBM/link paths.

architectureFlow.title: Memory and Batch
architectureFlow.summary: HBM budget, KV cache, expert weights, MLA weights, tile fit, and max batch estimates.

architectureFlow.title: Search and Scenarios
architectureFlow.summary: GPU count, PP/DP/TP/EP/CP, batch, MTP, PD, and Decode Attention/FFN separation sweeps.

architectureFlow.title: Outputs and Visualization
architectureFlow.summary: Excel details, CSV/JSON summaries, Pareto fronts, stage plots, and web dashboards.

scenarios.title: Prefill
scenarios.description: High token parallelism dominated by long prompt attention, FFN throughput, and prompt-stage communication.

scenarios.title: Decode
scenarios.description: Single-token or small-token generation dominated by KV cache traffic, MoE dispatch/combine, and GPU utilization.

scenarios.title: EAGLE MTP
scenarios.description: Speculative multi-token decode analysis with accept-rate and draft-path overhead.

scenarios.title: Prefill/Decode Disaggregation
scenarios.description: Separate prefill and decode hardware pools, transfer KV cache, and estimate system bottleneck stage.

scenarios.title: Decode Attention/FFN Separation
scenarios.description: Split decode attention and FFN work across hardware pools and account for activation transfer overhead.

sections.title: Cost Model Overall Architecture
sections.summary: The cost model is organized as a pipeline from model-layer inputs to hardware-aware operator estimates, communication estimates, capacity checks, strategy sweeps, and web-ready outputs.

analysisPoints.name: End-to-end cost pipeline
analysisPoints.what: Trace how a workbook or graph layer table becomes per-layer timing, summary rows, and visualization data.
analysisPoints.paperNote: This is the paper's main abstraction: model-layer tables and hardware specs become explainable timing, capacity, search, and visualization outputs.

analysisPoints.name: Layer-to-kernel dispatch
analysisPoints.what: Classify layer types and dispatch them to GEMM, MHA, Softmax, Vector, or Depthwise estimators.
analysisPoints.paperNote: The paper treats operator dispatch as the boundary between model topology and kernel-level cost formulas.

analysisPoints.name: Phase-level aggregation
analysisPoints.what: Aggregate dense/sparse layer schedules, communication, and optional MTP paths into model-level latency and throughput.
analysisPoints.paperNote: Aggregation is where dense/sparse layer costs, communication, and optional MTP paths become user-facing TPOT and throughput.

sections.title: Input Parameters and Modeled Objects
sections.summary: Inputs cover model topology, layer shapes, token lengths, precision, phase, MoE parameters, MTP controls, and disaggregated deployment options.

analysisPoints.name: Layer schema coverage
analysisPoints.what: Analyze each layer row using NN name, type, token, c_in/c_out, heads, experts, layout, precision, and parallel hints.
analysisPoints.paperNote: Covered by the paper's Input Parameters and Modeled Objects section as part of the cost-model analysis surface.

analysisPoints.name: Prefill and decode phase split
analysisPoints.what: Model prompt processing and generation separately because sequence shape, KV traffic, and throughput formulas differ.
analysisPoints.paperNote: The paper explicitly separates prefill and decode because prompt parallelism and generation KV pressure have different bottlenecks.

analysisPoints.name: MoE workload structure
analysisPoints.what: Capture shared experts, routed experts, top-k, dense layers, sparse layers, and EP communication behavior.
analysisPoints.paperNote: Covered by the paper's Input Parameters and Modeled Objects section as part of the cost-model analysis surface.

analysisPoints.name: Precision and byte-width analysis
analysisPoints.what: Vary activation, weight, output, and KV cache bytes for fp16/fp8/fp4-style analyses.
analysisPoints.paperNote: Covered by the paper's Input Parameters and Modeled Objects section as part of the cost-model analysis surface.

analysisPoints.name: Graph-derived model input
analysisPoints.what: Generate LLMPerf layer tables from Hugging Face or graph metadata for models not authored directly in Excel.
analysisPoints.paperNote: Covered by the paper's Input Parameters and Modeled Objects section as part of the cost-model analysis surface.

sections.title: Hardware Abstraction Layer
sections.summary: The hardware model maps GPU databases into topology, compute, storage, and link parameters used by operator and communication models.

analysisPoints.name: GPU family comparison
analysisPoints.what: Compare NVIDIA, AMD, and other accelerators using a unified row-based hardware table.
analysisPoints.paperNote: Hardware rows make what-if analysis possible across H100, H200, B200, B300, R200, R300, and other table-backed devices.

analysisPoints.name: Compute unit abstraction
analysisPoints.what: Model tensor-core tile sizes, vector lanes, SFU lanes, frequency, and effective SM count.
analysisPoints.paperNote: Covered by the paper's Hardware Abstraction Layer section as part of the cost-model analysis surface.

analysisPoints.name: Memory hierarchy abstraction
analysisPoints.what: Track RF, shared memory, TMEM, L2, and HBM capacities and bandwidths.
analysisPoints.paperNote: Covered by the paper's Hardware Abstraction Layer section as part of the cost-model analysis surface.

analysisPoints.name: Topology abstraction
analysisPoints.what: Represent die, partition, GPC, SM, L2 group, HBM section, link, and D2D topology.
analysisPoints.paperNote: Covered by the paper's Hardware Abstraction Layer section as part of the cost-model analysis surface.

analysisPoints.name: Interconnect abstraction
analysisPoints.what: Model P2P, PCIe, NIC, D2D, and intra-node or inter-node transport latency and bandwidth.
analysisPoints.paperNote: Covered by the paper's Hardware Abstraction Layer section as part of the cost-model analysis surface.

sections.title: Operator-Level Modeling
sections.summary: Operator models estimate compute time, memory traffic, tile choices, and bottlenecks for core transformer kernels.

analysisPoints.name: GEMM M/N/K/E mapping
analysisPoints.what: Convert Conv/GEMM/MatMul rows into GPU-local and SM-local M/N/K/E work shapes.
analysisPoints.paperNote: Covered by the paper's Operator-Level Modeling section as part of the cost-model analysis surface.

analysisPoints.name: GEMM tile and loop-order search
analysisPoints.what: Search legal SM tile shapes and loop orders to estimate compute and memory reload traffic.
analysisPoints.paperNote: The paper highlights tile legality, reload traffic, and loop order as the explainable layer below model-level timing.

analysisPoints.name: MHA and Flash Attention analysis
analysisPoints.what: Estimate QK, softmax auxiliary work, SV, KV load, and output traffic for flash and non-flash attention.
analysisPoints.paperNote: Attention analysis separates QK, softmax, SV, KV load, and CP effects so long-context bottlenecks remain inspectable.

analysisPoints.name: Softmax SIMT/SFU analysis
analysisPoints.what: Estimate score-matrix max/sum/scale passes and SFU exp pressure for flash and non-flash softmax.
analysisPoints.paperNote: Covered by the paper's Operator-Level Modeling section as part of the cost-model analysis surface.

analysisPoints.name: Vector and normalization analysis
analysisPoints.what: Estimate vector-style operators such as RMSNorm, RoPE, and elementwise transforms.
analysisPoints.paperNote: Covered by the paper's Operator-Level Modeling section as part of the cost-model analysis surface.

analysisPoints.name: Depthwise convolution analysis
analysisPoints.what: Estimate depthwise convolution cost separately from dense GEMM-style convolution.
analysisPoints.paperNote: Covered by the paper's Operator-Level Modeling section as part of the cost-model analysis surface.

sections.title: Communication and NoC Modeling
sections.summary: The communication layer estimates collective communication and on-chip traffic from operator work maps and parallel strategy.

analysisPoints.name: Tensor parallel communication
analysisPoints.what: Estimate all-reduce, all-gather, and reduce-scatter traffic required by TP splits.
analysisPoints.paperNote: TP communication is one of the main collective costs included in the paper's communication layer.

analysisPoints.name: Expert parallel dispatch/combine
analysisPoints.what: Estimate MoE all-to-all style dispatch and combine communication.
analysisPoints.paperNote: MoE routing overhead is modeled through dispatch and combine traffic rather than hidden inside FFN compute.

analysisPoints.name: Pipeline parallel communication
analysisPoints.what: Model activation send/receive at selected stage boundaries.
analysisPoints.paperNote: Covered by the paper's Communication and NoC Modeling section as part of the cost-model analysis surface.

analysisPoints.name: Context parallel attention communication
analysisPoints.what: Model KV sequence sharding and ring-attention communication for attention CP.
analysisPoints.paperNote: Covered by the paper's Communication and NoC Modeling section as part of the cost-model analysis surface.

analysisPoints.name: On-chip NoC traffic
analysisPoints.what: Route SM, GPC, L2, HBM, link, and D2D traffic according to work maps.
analysisPoints.paperNote: NoC routing is the bridge from operator bytes to SM/GPC/L2/HBM/link bottleneck explanations.

analysisPoints.name: Communication algorithm selection
analysisPoints.what: Compare all-reduce modes such as ring, SUMMA, tree, and all-to-all variants.
analysisPoints.paperNote: Covered by the paper's Communication and NoC Modeling section as part of the cost-model analysis surface.

sections.title: Memory Capacity and Batch Constraints
sections.summary: Capacity modeling estimates HBM budget for weights, experts, MLA parameters, KV cache, and activation/tile storage.

analysisPoints.name: HBM budget
analysisPoints.what: Subtract model weights, expert weights, MLA parameters, and KV cache from available HBM.
analysisPoints.paperNote: The paper treats capacity as a first-class rejection rule before throughput analysis.

analysisPoints.name: Force max batch
analysisPoints.what: Estimate maximum decode batch from remaining HBM and per-request KV cache cost.
analysisPoints.paperNote: Covered by the paper's Memory Capacity and Batch Constraints section as part of the cost-model analysis surface.

analysisPoints.name: KV cache footprint
analysisPoints.what: Estimate per-batch KV cache using compressed KV, RoPE dimension, KV heads, layer count, sequence length, and bpe.
analysisPoints.paperNote: This metric connects decode capacity and PD transfer cost through the same KV-cache shape assumptions.

analysisPoints.name: Tile fit constraints
analysisPoints.what: Reject or downselect SM tile candidates that exceed RF, shared memory, or local buffer capacity.
analysisPoints.paperNote: Covered by the paper's Memory Capacity and Batch Constraints section as part of the cost-model analysis surface.

analysisPoints.name: Activation and output traffic
analysisPoints.what: Estimate per-layer activation, weight, and output memory movement under chosen tiling and layout.
analysisPoints.paperNote: Covered by the paper's Memory Capacity and Batch Constraints section as part of the cost-model analysis surface.

sections.title: Parallel Strategy Search
sections.summary: The model can sweep system-level and kernel-level strategies to find Pareto-efficient latency-throughput points.

analysisPoints.name: System map enumeration
analysisPoints.what: Enumerate legal PP, attention DP/TP/CP, and FFN DP/TP/EP combinations under a fixed GPU count.
analysisPoints.paperNote: System search enumerates exact-fit PP, attention DP/TP/CP, and FFN DP/TP/EP mappings.

analysisPoints.name: GPU work-map enumeration
analysisPoints.what: Generate die/partition/GPC/SM work maps for active SM counts.
analysisPoints.paperNote: Covered by the paper's Parallel Strategy Search section as part of the cost-model analysis surface.

analysisPoints.name: Batch and GPU count sweep
analysisPoints.what: Run performance sweeps across GPU counts, batch sizes, and parallel settings.
analysisPoints.paperNote: Covered by the paper's Parallel Strategy Search section as part of the cost-model analysis surface.

analysisPoints.name: Operator micro-sweep
analysisPoints.what: Scan GEMM M/N/K or MHA Q/KV/hidden dimensions to isolate kernel-level behavior.
analysisPoints.paperNote: Covered by the paper's Parallel Strategy Search section as part of the cost-model analysis surface.

analysisPoints.name: Pareto frontier analysis
analysisPoints.what: Identify non-dominated points trading off user latency and total throughput.
analysisPoints.paperNote: The paper uses Pareto analysis to explain trade-offs between per-user latency and cluster throughput.

sections.title: Prefill, Decode, MTP, and PD Scenario Analysis
sections.summary: Scenario-level analysis combines phase summaries, deployment split choices, and throughput constraints into architecture decisions.

analysisPoints.name: Prefill stage behavior
analysisPoints.what: Analyze prompt throughput, long-sequence attention cost, and prompt-stage compute/communication mix.
analysisPoints.paperNote: Prefill analysis focuses on prompt throughput and long-context attention/FFN work.

analysisPoints.name: Decode stage behavior
analysisPoints.what: Analyze per-user generation TPS, KV load, MoE communication, and batch-dependent GPU utilization.
analysisPoints.paperNote: Decode analysis focuses on TPOT, user TPS, KV movement, MoE communication, and utilization.

analysisPoints.name: EAGLE MTP stage sweep
analysisPoints.what: Estimate accepted-token speedup against draft-path overhead across MTP stages and accept rates.
analysisPoints.paperNote: MTP analysis combines expected accepted tokens with draft, verify, and memory overhead ratios.

analysisPoints.name: Prefill/Decode disaggregation
analysisPoints.what: Combine separate prefill and decode phase results and estimate system throughput under KV transfer constraints.
analysisPoints.paperNote: PD analysis adds KV transfer as an explicit pipeline stage between independent prefill and decode pools.

analysisPoints.name: Same-node vs cross-node PD
analysisPoints.what: Select NVLink/P2P when phases fit one node and CX/NIC when they cross nodes.
analysisPoints.paperNote: Placement chooses local P2P/NVLink or cross-node NIC transport depending on phase allocation.

sections.title: Decode Attention/FFN Separation
sections.summary: Decode Attention/FFN separation models a decode deployment where attention and FFN stages may run on separate hardware pools with activation transfer between them.

analysisPoints.name: Attention/FFN split activation transfer
analysisPoints.what: Estimate activation bytes sent between decode attention and decode FFN pools.
analysisPoints.paperNote: The paper's decode A/F formula makes the boundary activation payload explicit.

analysisPoints.name: Split-stage bottleneck analysis
analysisPoints.what: Treat attention, FFN, and activation transfer as separate pipeline stages and choose the slowest stage as system bottleneck.
analysisPoints.paperNote: Decode A/F split is interpreted as attention, FFN, and transfer stages with the slowest stage limiting RPS.

analysisPoints.name: Transport sensitivity
analysisPoints.what: Compare CX/NIC, PCIe, NVLink, or user-provided bandwidth/latency for the attention-to-FFN boundary.
analysisPoints.paperNote: The paper calls out bandwidth, latency, placement, and GPU-table transport assumptions as sensitivity knobs.

analysisPoints.name: Layer-count sensitivity
analysisPoints.what: Evaluate how more decoder layers or repeated boundary transfers scale activation movement.
analysisPoints.paperNote: Covered by the paper's Decode Attention/FFN Separation section as part of the cost-model analysis surface.

analysisPoints.name: Architecture decision support
analysisPoints.what: Identify when splitting decode attention and FFN improves stage utilization or becomes network-bound.
analysisPoints.paperNote: The decision point is whether A/F split improves utilization or simply moves the bottleneck onto the network.

sections.title: Output Metrics and Visualization
sections.summary: Outputs include per-config Excel files, aggregated CSV/JSON, Pareto payloads, plots, and static web dashboards.

analysisPoints.name: Per-layer Excel result
analysisPoints.what: Write each simulated configuration to a workbook with layer timing and final summary rows.
analysisPoints.paperNote: Covered by the paper's Output Metrics and Visualization section as part of the cost-model analysis surface.

analysisPoints.name: Summary CSV/JSON
analysisPoints.what: Aggregate many result files into analysis tables consumed by scripts and web pages.
analysisPoints.paperNote: Covered by the paper's Output Metrics and Visualization section as part of the cost-model analysis surface.

analysisPoints.name: Latency-throughput Pareto visualization
analysisPoints.what: Plot latency-sensitive TPS per user against total throughput and expose frontier parameter deltas.
analysisPoints.paperNote: The web Pareto view exposes frontier rows and parameter deltas for planning decisions.

analysisPoints.name: Stage decomposition profiles
analysisPoints.what: Break total time into MLA, KV load, dense MLP, dispatch/combine, experts, all-reduce, and PP/CP communication.
analysisPoints.paperNote: Profile views map TPOT back to MLA, KV load, MLP, dispatch/combine, experts, and communication.

analysisPoints.name: Disaggregation plots
analysisPoints.what: Visualize PD transfer time, decode A/F transfer time, transfer bytes, and stage rps.
analysisPoints.paperNote: Disaggregation plots are the paper's visual output for PD and decode A/F transfer sensitivity.

sections.title: Model Limitations and Applicability Boundaries
sections.summary: The model is useful for architecture analysis and strategy comparison, but it is not a cycle-accurate simulator or a replacement for profiling real kernels.

analysisPoints.name: Analytical approximation boundary
analysisPoints.what: Use the model for relative comparisons and bottleneck discovery, not exact kernel runtime prediction.
analysisPoints.paperNote: The paper frames all estimates as analytical planning signals, not cycle-accurate predictions.

analysisPoints.name: Hardware data dependency
analysisPoints.what: Accuracy depends on GPU table parameters, bandwidth assumptions, and latency constants.
analysisPoints.paperNote: Covered by the paper's Model Limitations and Applicability Boundaries section as part of the cost-model analysis surface.

analysisPoints.name: Kernel implementation dependency
analysisPoints.what: Actual vendor kernel scheduling, fusion, caching, and compiler behavior may differ from modeled tiles.
analysisPoints.paperNote: Covered by the paper's Model Limitations and Applicability Boundaries section as part of the cost-model analysis surface.

analysisPoints.name: Overlap and scheduling assumptions
analysisPoints.what: Layer aggregation uses explicit overlap rules for sparse attention/expert branches and may not capture all runtime scheduling effects.
analysisPoints.paperNote: Covered by the paper's Model Limitations and Applicability Boundaries section as part of the cost-model analysis surface.

analysisPoints.name: Validation workflow
analysisPoints.what: Treat outputs as hypotheses to validate with targeted kernel benchmarks, communication microbenchmarks, and end-to-end profiling.
analysisPoints.paperNote: The recommended use is narrow with LLMPerf, validate with microbenchmarks/profiles, then calibrate constants.

paperOutline.title: Input Parameters and Modeled Objects
paperOutline.summary: Layer rows and model configuration define operator type, tensor shape, phase, precision, MoE topology, token lengths, and optional deployment controls.

paperOutline.title: Hardware Abstraction Layer
paperOutline.summary: GPU table rows become compute, topology, memory, and transport objects used by every estimator.

paperOutline.title: Operator-Level Modeling
paperOutline.summary: Core kernels estimate compute cycles, legal tiles, reload traffic, local memory movement, and operator bottlenecks.

paperOutline.title: Communication and NoC Modeling
paperOutline.summary: The communication layer accounts for inter-GPU collectives and on-chip routes through SM, GPC, L2, HBM, links, and D2D paths.

paperOutline.title: Memory Capacity and Batch Constraints
paperOutline.summary: Capacity analysis subtracts weights, expert state, MLA parameters, and KV cache before a strategy is accepted.

paperOutline.title: Parallel Strategy Search
paperOutline.summary: Search spans GPU count, batch, hardware type, PP, attention DP/TP/CP, FFN DP/TP/EP, and kernel-local tile choices.

paperOutline.title: Prefill, Decode, MTP, and PD Scenario Analysis
paperOutline.summary: Scenario models combine phase summaries with deployment choices to explain throughput, latency, and bottleneck movement.

paperOutline.title: Decode Attention/FFN Separation
paperOutline.summary: Decode A/F separation models attention and FFN as separate hardware pools connected by activation transfer.

paperOutline.title: Output Metrics and Visualization
paperOutline.summary: The framework writes audit-friendly workbooks and web-ready CSV/JSON used for Pareto, phase profile, MTP, and disaggregation views.

paperOutline.title: Model Limitations and Applicability Boundaries
paperOutline.summary: The paper positions LLMPerf as an analytical planning model, not a cycle-accurate simulator or replacement for profiling.

formulas.name: KV cache footprint / PD transfer payload
formulas.expression: B * T_in * L * (compress_kv + rope_dim) * head_kv * kv_cache_bpe
formulas.meaning: Approximates the per-request KV payload transferred from prefill to decode in PD disaggregation.

formulas.name: Decode Attention/FFN activation transfer
formulas.expression: B * T_out * L * H * b_act * m
formulas.meaning: Activation bytes crossing the decode attention-to-FFN boundary; m is transfers per layer.

formulas.name: EAGLE MTP expected accepted tokens
formulas.meaning: Effective decode tokens per step before draft, verify, and memory overhead ratios are applied.

formulas.name: Total throughput
formulas.meaning: Cluster-level throughput metric used as the Pareto y-axis when available.

formulas.name: Disaggregated system request rate
formulas.meaning: Pipeline-style bottleneck rule for PD and decode A/F split scenario analyses.

formulas.name: Pareto dominance rule
formulas.meaning: A point remains on the frontier when no other row improves both latency-sensitive user TPS and total throughput.

keyMetrics.name: TPOT
keyMetrics.meaning: Time per output token; the main decode latency metric.

keyMetrics.name: TPS per user
keyMetrics.meaning: User-visible generation rate derived from TPOT.

keyMetrics.name: TPS per GPU
keyMetrics.meaning: Normalized hardware throughput for comparing strategies.

keyMetrics.name: Total throughput
keyMetrics.meaning: Cluster throughput across all GPUs; Pareto y-axis for strategy sweeps.

keyMetrics.name: Compute time
keyMetrics.meaning: Aggregated compute contribution from operator estimators.

keyMetrics.name: HBM remaining
keyMetrics.meaning: Post-weight and KV-cache memory budget used to reject infeasible rows.

keyMetrics.name: MLA/FFN all-reduce time
keyMetrics.meaning: Collective communication contribution from attention and FFN tensor parallelism.

keyMetrics.name: CP communication time
keyMetrics.meaning: Ring-attention or context-parallel KV exchange time.

keyMetrics.name: Dominant component
keyMetrics.meaning: Largest modeled component used for bottleneck explanation.

keyMetrics.name: PD transfer time
keyMetrics.meaning: KV transfer latency between prefill and decode pools.

keyMetrics.name: Decode A/F transfer time
keyMetrics.meaning: Activation transfer latency between decode attention and FFN pools.

keyMetrics.name: MTP expected tokens
keyMetrics.meaning: Accepted-token gain from speculative decode stages and accept rate.
